title: Define the user flow and design direction
summary: Translate the PRD into an actual interaction model and visual direction before implementation sprawls.
acceptance: Primary user flow is written in order from entry to successful completion.
Design direction describes layout, tone, density, and interaction style.
Unknowns are reduced to a small list of explicit decisions.
notes: When the product needs UI, plan the empty, loading, error, and success states.
Keep the direction specific enough that the UI will not default to generic AI styling.
